selected_problem_solved: The proof establishes the abstract finite-cover upgrade lemma: assuming a separable tracial von Neumann algebra \(M\) lies in the stated DKEP framework, has no amenable direct summand, has boundary-piece support projections \(q_U\) for all finite intersections with \(q_\varnothing=q_K\), satisfies \(q_Uq_Wq_U\le q_{U\cap W}\), and satisfies the stated DKEP obstruction theorem, then relative proper proximality of \(M\) with respect to each \(X_{U_i}\) and \(\bigcap_i U_i=\varnothing\) imply that \(M\) is properly proximal.
selector_skipped_reason: N_PARALLEL_RUNS=1 — single run, no selection needed

all_runs.label: A
all_runs.status: Track A finalize complete (full-proof polish, seed=nearly_verified_full_proof). See solution.tex and benchmark.json.
all_runs.problem_solved: The proof establishes the abstract finite-cover upgrade lemma: assuming a separable tracial von Neumann algebra \(M\) lies in the stated DKEP framework, has no amenable direct summand, has boundary-piece support projections \(q_U\) for all finite intersections with \(q_\varnothing=q_K\), satisfies \(q_Uq_Wq_U\le q_{U\cap W}\), and satisfies the stated DKEP obstruction theorem, then relative proper proximality of \(M\) with respect to each \(X_{U_i}\) and \(\bigcap_i U_i=\varnothing\) imply that \(M\) is properly proximal.
